criteria.evidence: meta-tests/test-git.bats exists (470 lines). Starts with '#!/usr/bin/env bats' shebang. Contains "load 'helpers/test-helper'" at line 8.
'bats meta-tests/test-git.bats' exits 0. All 25 tests show 'ok'. Zero 'not ok' results.
'bash meta-tests/run.sh' exits 0. All 83 tests pass across 4 test files (test-git, test-infrastructure, test-utils-pure, test-utils-state). Completed in 9s.

25 @test blocks counted (>= 20 threshold). Covers all 6 git.sh functions: git_create_harness_branch (4), git_create_sprint_branch (4), git_merge_sprint (6), git_fail_sprint_attempt (4), git_commit_harness_state (3), generate_pr_body (4).

Test 'git_create_harness_branch: creates a harness/{slug} branch from main' (line 27) calls git_create_harness_branch with 'my-project', then asserts branch 'harness/my-project' exists via git rev-parse --verify. Passes.
Test 'git_create_harness_branch: returns branch name on stdout' (line 37) uses 'run --separate-stderr' to capture output and asserts '$output == "harness/test-slug"'. Passes.

Test 'git_create_harness_branch: is idempotent...' (line 46) calls function twice with 'idempotent-test'. Second call succeeds (exit 0), output matches, and git branch --show-current confirms correct branch. Passes.
Test 'git_create_sprint_branch: creates branch with zero-padded sprint number' (line 81) passes sprint number 3 and verifies 'harness/my-project-sprint-03' exists. Passes.
Test 'git_create_sprint_branch: returns branch name on stdout and leaves repo on sprint branch' (line 92) checks output == 'harness/my-project-sprint-05' and verifies current branch matches. Passes.

Test at line 107 pre-creates a stale sprint branch with a commit, advances harness HEAD, then calls git_create_sprint_branch. Asserts new sprint branch HEAD matches harness HEAD (not stale SHA). Passes.

Test 'git_merge_sprint: performs a --no-ff merge...' (line 151) sets up harness+sprint branches, commits on sprint, calls git_merge_sprint. Verifies current branch is harness and merge commit message contains 'harness(sprint-01)'. Passes.
Test at line 174 calls git_merge_sprint then verifies 'harness/sprint-01/pass' tag exists. Asserts tag SHA equals HEAD SHA on harness branch. Passes.
Test at line 195 calls git_merge_sprint then runs 'git rev-parse --verify harness/my-project-sprint-01' which fails (status != 0), confirming branch is deleted. Passes.
Test at line 211 captures output of git_merge_sprint with '--separate-stderr'. Verifies output contains HEAD SHA of harness branch. Passes.
Test at line 230 creates committed work + uncommitted 'uncommitted.txt' on sprint branch, calls git_merge_sprint. Verifies file exists and content matches on harness branch after merge. Passes.
Test at line 269 sets up sprint branch, commits, calls git_fail_sprint_attempt with attempt=2. Verifies tag 'harness/sprint-01/attempt-2' exists via git rev-parse. Passes.
Test at line 284 calls git_fail_sprint_attempt. Asserts current branch is harness branch and sprint branch no longer exists (rev-parse fails). Passes.
Test at line 305 creates tracked file, commits it, modifies it (dirty tree), calls git_fail_sprint_attempt. Exit 0 confirmed. 'git status --porcelain' returns empty (clean working directory). Passes.
Test at line 355 writes to $HARNESS_STATE/config.json, calls git_commit_harness_state. Verifies git log -1 message matches. Passes.
Test at line 369 records commit count before, calls git_commit_harness_state with no changes, verifies commit count unchanged. Passes.
Test at line 382 creates initial tracked file, commits it, then modifies it AND adds new untracked file. Calls git_commit_harness_state. Verifies both files appear in git diff-tree output. Passes.
Test at line 409 installs config and sprint-plan fixtures, calls generate_pr_body, greps output for exact table header '| Sprint | Name | Status | Criteria | Pass | Fail | Attempts | Cost |'. Passes.
Test at line 423 installs eval-report-pass.json fixture for sprint 1, calls generate_pr_body. Output contains 'PASS'. Passes.
Test at line 440 provides eval report only for sprint 1 of a 3-sprint plan. Output contains 'pending' for sprints without reports. Passes.
Test at line 457 calls generate_pr_body. Verifies output contains 'opus', 'reset', and 'web-app' from config-valid.json fixture. Passes.

All 25 @test blocks call init_test_repo (25 matches confirmed by grep). setup() creates fresh TEST_TEMP_DIR per test via mktemp -d. No test references branches or tags that only exist if another test ran first.

All 25 test names follow 'function_name: descriptive behavior' convention. Examples: 'git_create_harness_branch: creates a harness/{slug} branch from main', 'git_merge_sprint: performs a --no-ff merge of sprint branch into harness branch', 'generate_pr_body: shows pending status for sprints without eval reports'.
setup() at lines 19-20 uses source_harness_lib 'utils.sh' and source_harness_lib 'git.sh'. No hardcoded paths to harness/lib/git.sh. Line 2 mentions harness/lib/git.sh in a comment only.

regression.details: Full suite 'bash meta-tests/run.sh' exits 0 with all 83 tests passing. Sprint 1 infrastructure intact (run.sh executable, helpers/, fixtures/ with 23 fixture files). Sprint 2 tests (test-utils-pure.bats, 22 tests) all pass. Sprint 3 tests (test-utils-state.bats, 28 tests) all pass.

dimensions.name: Correctness & Reliability
dimensions.rationale: All 25 tests pass on first run. Tests correctly validate all 6 git.sh functions with meaningful assertions that verify actual git state (branch existence, HEAD SHA, tag resolution, working tree cleanliness) rather than just exit codes.

dimensions.name: Error Handling & Edge Cases
dimensions.rationale: Covers key edge cases: idempotent branch creation, stale branch cleanup, dirty working tree handling, no-op commit behavior, double-digit sprint numbers, multiple sequential failed attempts. Edge cases like invalid inputs are explicitly out of scope per contract (sprint 6).

dimensions.name: Code Quality & Maintainability
dimensions.rationale: Well-organized with clear section headers per function. Consistent patterns across all tests: init_test_repo at top, run --separate-stderr for output capture, explicit assertions. Uses helper abstractions (source_harness_lib, install_fixture, init_test_repo). No code duplication.

dimensions.name: Test Quality & Coverage
dimensions.rationale: 25 tests covering all 6 public functions with 3-6 tests each. Tests verify behavior not implementation (checks git state post-call, not internal function mechanics). Each test is isolated via mktemp -d + init_test_repo. Multi-step scenarios validate complex workflows (create, advance, recreate, verify).

dimensions.name: Documentation & Usability
dimensions.rationale: File header describes coverage scope across all 6 functions. Section headers organize tests logically. Test names are self-documenting and follow consistent convention. Comments explain non-obvious setups. Minor gap: complex multi-assertion tests could benefit from inline comments explaining what each assertion verifies.

dimensions.name: Performance
dimensions.rationale: Full 83-test suite completes in 9 seconds including 25 new git-heavy tests. Each test uses lightweight temp directories with small repos (1-3 commits). No unnecessary waits, sleeps, or retries.
